prompt:
You are Agent 2 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-4X4jPG.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Implement the email notification adapter fully.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification architecture from the previous session (check src/services/, src/adapters/, src/events/, and any design docs).
2. Implement the email adapter with real logic: template rendering, recipient resolution, send method.
3. Wire the email adapter into the notification service following the established pattern.
4. Add basic unit tests for the email adapter.
5. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Follow the architecture from Session 1 exactly — do not change the notification pattern.
- The email adapter must conform to whatever adapter interface was defined.

parameters.task: Implement email notification adapter with template rendering, recipient resolution, send method. Wire into notification service. Add unit tests.

parameters.description: List test files and root directory

parameters.old_string:
export { EmailAdapter } from './adapters/email.adapter.js';
export type { EmailAdapterConfig } from './adapters/email.adapter.js';
parameters.new_string:
export { EmailAdapter, InMemoryTransport, FailingTransport } from './adapters/email.adapter.js';
export type { EmailAdapterConfig, EmailTransport, RenderedEmail } from './adapters/email.adapter.js';

parameters.summary: Email adapter uses transport abstraction with template rendering and recipient validation
parameters.context: The Session 1 EmailAdapter was a stub that logged in-memory. Need to implement real email logic with template rendering, recipient resolution, and configurable sending.
parameters.rationale: Introduced EmailTransport interface to decouple sending from the adapter logic. This allows InMemoryTransport for testing and real providers (SES, SendGrid) for production without changing adapter code. Templates are defined per NotificationType with HTML and plain text variants. Recipient validation normalizes and validates emails before sending. Errors are caught and returned as NotificationResult failures rather than thrown.

alternatives.option: Keep stub adapter and mock in tests only
alternatives.reason_rejected: Task explicitly requires real template rendering, recipient resolution, and send logic
No external dependencies in this project; inline templates are sufficient for the 4 notification types

parameters.summary: Agent-2: Email adapter fully implemented with transport abstraction, template rendering, recipient validation, and 22 unit tests
parameters.detail:
Replaced the stub EmailAdapter with a full implementation:

ARCHITECTURE:
- EmailTransport interface decouples sending from adapter logic (InMemoryTransport for tests, real providers for production)
- Template rendering per NotificationType (order:confirmation, order:status-update, user:welcome, user:profile-updated) with HTML + plain text
- Recipient resolution: validates and normalizes email addresses (trim, lowercase, regex check)
- HTML escaping in templates to prevent XSS
- Errors caught and returned as NotificationResult failures (no thrown exceptions from send())

MODIFIED FILES:
- src/adapters/email.adapter.ts — Full rewrite: EmailTransport interface, InMemoryTransport, FailingTransport, template rendering, recipient validation
- src/index.ts — Added exports for InMemoryTransport, FailingTransport, EmailTransport, RenderedEmail

NEW FILES:
- tests/adapters/email.adapter.test.ts — 22 tests covering: recipient resolution, template rendering for all 4 types, XSS escaping, send success/failure, transport behavior

PRESERVED:
- NotificationAdapter interface contract (channel + send method)
- EmailAdapterConfig interface (extended with transport field)
- All 70 existing tests still pass (92 total now)

TypeScript compiles cleanly. All 92 tests pass.
